steps.title: Use the large triangle first
steps.content:
In the figure, $U$, $V$, and $R$ are collinear, and $T$ lies on $\overline{US}$. The given $34^\circ$ angle is $\angle RVS$, so the interior angle $\angle UVS$ is supplementary to it:
$$\angle UVS=180^\circ-34^\circ=146^\circ.$$

steps.title: Find the base angle at U
steps.content:
Triangle $UVS$ has angles $146^\circ$ at $V$ and $23^\circ$ at $S$, so the angle at $U$ is:
$$180^\circ-146^\circ-23^\circ=11^\circ.$$
Because $T$ is on $\overline{US}$ and $R$ is on $\overline{UV}$, this is also $\angle TUR$.

steps.title: Use the isosceles triangle
steps.content: The question gives $RT=TU$, so triangle $TRU$ is isosceles. Its base angles at $R$ and $U$ are equal, so both base angles are $11^\circ$.

steps.title: Compute x
steps.content:
The angle marked $x^\circ$ is the vertex angle $\angle UTR$ of triangle $TRU$:
$$x=180^\circ-11^\circ-11^\circ=158^\circ.$$
Therefore, $x=\boxed{158}$.
